title: silene uniflora subsp. uniflora
intro: Silene uniflora subsp. uniflora, also known as the one-flowered catchfly, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues. The plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or in the form of a tincture, to harness its medicinal properties. Additionally, the plant's leaves and flowers are sometimes used in folk remedies to treat various health conditions, although its efficacy has not been extensively scientifically proven.
